package service

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"net/http"
)

// Positive scenario for service_depends_on_transport_request_type:
// service signature accepts *http.Request instead of domain inputs.
type UserService struct {
	repo UserRepository
}

type UserRepository interface {
	FindByEmail(ctx context.Context, email string) (*User, error)
}

type User struct {
	ID    string
	Email string
	Name  string
}

func NewUserService(repo UserRepository) *UserService {
	return &UserService{repo: repo}
}

func (s *UserService) GetUserFromRequest(ctx context.Context, r *http.Request) (*User, int, error) {
	authHeader := r.Header.Get("Authorization")
	if authHeader == "" {
		return nil, http.StatusUnauthorized, fmt.Errorf("missing authorization header")
	}

	email := r.URL.Query().Get("email")
	if email == "" {
		return nil, http.StatusBadRequest, fmt.Errorf("email query parameter required")
	}

	user, err := s.repo.FindByEmail(ctx, email)
	if err != nil {
		return nil, http.StatusNotFound, err
	}

	body, _ := io.ReadAll(r.Body)
	_ = body

	return user, http.StatusOK, nil
}
